headline_marked: [[Tere Naam]] and [[Rangeela]] sequels announced by Eros
headline: Tere Naam and Rangeela sequels announced by Eros
summary: Eros International unveils plans for sequels to iconic Bollywood films "Tere Naam" and "Rangeela" as part of its new Eros Universe initiative. The company also announced new projects inspired by mythology and a restoration project for "Kochadaiiyaan".
details: Indian entertainment company Eros International has announced a major content slate, including sequel plans for popular Bollywood films "Tere Naam" and "Rangeela". The announcement was made during London Tech Week as part of the company’s new Eros Universe initiative, aiming to expand well-known film properties into new franchise stories.

The upcoming slate includes sequels to "Tanu Weds Manu – The Next Chapter", "Phobia", "English Vinglish", "Desi Boyz", "Rangeela", and "Tere Naam". While sequels for "Rangeela" and "Tere Naam" are officially in development, the company has not confirmed if the original stars will return.

Salman Khan is unlikely to reprise his iconic role as Radhe in a "Tere Naam" follow-up, and there is no confirmation of Aamir Khan or Urmila Matondkar's involvement in a new "Rangeela". "Rangeela", directed by Ram Gopal Varma, was a defining musical hit of the 1990s, while "Tere Naam", directed by the late Satish Kaushik, is acclaimed for Salman Khan's performance.

The "Tanu Weds Manu – The Next Chapter" project, to be directed by Mitakshara Kumar, is expected to begin production in 2026. The company also revealed Eros Brahmand, a mythology-inspired cinematic universe with nine original titles, and Eros Remastered, a restoration initiative starting with Rajinikanth's motion-capture film "Kochadaiiyaan".
Eros founder and chairman Kishore Lulla stated the company's aim to unite mythology-inspired stories, beloved film properties, and classic cinema under a single creative vision.
caption:
Sequels to Bollywood classics "Tere Naam" and "Rangeela" are officially in development.
Eros International announces new franchise expansion plans.

Indian entertainment company Eros International has announced a major content slate, including sequel plans for popular Bollywood films "Tere Naam" and "Rangeela". The announcement was made during London Tech Week as part of the company’s new Eros Universe initiative, aiming to expand well-known film properties into new franchise stories.

The upcoming slate includes sequels to "Tanu Weds Manu – The Next Chapter", "Phobia", "English Vinglish", "Desi Boyz", "Rangeela", and "Tere Naam". While sequels for "Rangeela" and "Tere Naam" are officially in development, the company has not confirmed if the original stars will return.

Salman Khan is unlikely to reprise his iconic role as Radhe in a "Tere Naam" follow-up, and there is no confirmation of Aamir Khan or Urmila Matondkar's involvement in a new "Rangeela". "Rangeela", directed by Ram Gopal Varma, was a defining musical hit of the 1990s, while "Tere Naam", directed by the late Satish Kaushik, is acclaimed for Salman Khan's performance.

The "Tanu Weds Manu – The Next Chapter" project, to be directed by Mitakshara Kumar, is expected to begin production in 2026. The company also revealed Eros Brahmand, a mythology-inspired cinematic universe with nine original titles, and Eros Remastered, a restoration initiative starting with Rajinikanth's motion-capture film "Kochadaiiyaan".

Eros founder and chairman Kishore Lulla stated the company's aim to unite mythology-inspired stories, beloved film properties, and classic cinema under a single creative vision.

#Bollywood #IndianCinema #TereNaam #Rangeela #ErosInternational

Source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News, Jamuna TV, The Daily Star
tweet: Sequels for iconic Bollywood films "Tere Naam" and "Rangeela" are in the works as part of Eros International's new "Eros Universe" initiative. The company also announced mythology-inspired projects and film restorations. #Bollywood #IndianCinema
source: Prothom Alo, Somoy News, Jamuna TV, The Daily Star

headline_marked: [[Bangladesh Cabinet]] approves record Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget for FY2026-27
headline: Bangladesh Cabinet approves record Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget for FY2026-27
summary: The BNP government's first budget in two decades, totaling Tk 9.38 lakh crore, has been approved by the cabinet. It aims to revive the economy and transform Bangladesh into a trillion-dollar economy by 2034.
details: The proposed national budget for fiscal year 2026-27, set at a record Tk 9.38 lakh crore, has been approved by the cabinet. This represents a 19% increase over the current revised budget, with a revenue target of Tk 6,95,000 crore.

The budget projects a fiscal deficit of approximately Tk 2.43 lakh crore, which will be financed through domestic bank borrowing and external creditors, with an ongoing negotiation for an IMF program.

This budget is presented amidst significant economic challenges, including a revenue shortfall, high non-performing loans in the banking sector, and increased energy import costs due to geopolitical events.

Key proposals include incentives for green energy, electric vehicles, freelancers, technology manufacturers, and healthcare, alongside increased taxes on tobacco and conventional fuel vehicles. Relief on essential goods is also planned to combat inflation.

The government has outlined a '3R strategy' for economic revival: Recovery & Stabilisation, Restoration, and Reconstruction for Acceleration, aiming to achieve the goal of a $1 trillion economy by 2034.
In a move to boost the stock market, the budget is expected to announce a gradual reduction in share trading settlement time, moving from the current T+2 to T+0 in phases.

Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury will also propose a complete exemption of VAT and advance tax at the import stage for all fertilisers and pesticides, as well as exempting VAT at the business stage for fertilisers and advance tax for pesticides.
caption:
Bangladesh's new national budget for FY2026-27 has been approved, totaling a record Tk 9.38 lakh crore.
Key proposals include incentives for green energy and relief on essential goods.

The proposed national budget for fiscal year 2026-27, set at a record Tk 9.38 lakh crore, has been approved by the cabinet. This represents a 19% increase over the current revised budget, with a revenue target of Tk 6,95,000 crore.

The budget projects a fiscal deficit of approximately Tk 2.43 lakh crore, which will be financed through domestic bank borrowing and external creditors, with an ongoing negotiation for an IMF program.

This budget is presented amidst significant economic challenges, including a revenue shortfall, high non-performing loans in the banking sector, and increased energy import costs due to geopolitical events.

Key proposals include incentives for green energy, electric vehicles, freelancers, technology manufacturers, and healthcare, alongside increased taxes on tobacco and conventional fuel vehicles. Relief on essential goods is also planned to combat inflation.

The government has outlined a '3R strategy' for economic revival: Recovery & Stabilisation, Restoration, and Reconstruction for Acceleration, aiming to achieve the goal of a $1 trillion economy by 2034.

In a move to boost the stock market, the budget is expected to announce a gradual reduction in share trading settlement time, moving from the current T+2 to T+0 in phases.

Finance Minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury will also propose a complete exemption of VAT and advance tax at the import stage for all fertilisers and pesticides, as well as exempting VAT at the business stage for fertilisers and advance tax for pesticides.

#BangladeshBudget #FY2026-27 #Economy #Finance #Dhaka #BudgetApproval

Source: Somoy News, Jamuna TV, Prothom Alo, The Daily Star
tweet: Bangladesh's cabinet approves record Tk 9.38 lakh crore budget for FY2026-27. The plan aims to revive the economy and boost green energy. #BangladeshBudget #Economy
